commit.message:
Rename misleading input_rank param to input_dim_value in processSlice… (#7739)

This pull request improves the handling of the `Slice` operator when
applied to empty or scalar-shaped tensors, ensuring correct shape
inference and preventing potential crashes. The changes include both a
bug fix in the shape inference logic and the addition of a targeted test
case.

**Bug fix for `Slice` shape inference:**

* Updated the `processSliceInputs` function in `defs.cc` to use the
actual input dimension size or value (instead of input rank) when
calculating slice bounds, ensuring correct handling for empty or scalar
dimensions.

**Testing improvements:**

* Added a new test `test_slice_scalar_shape_output` in
`shape_inference_test.py` to verify that slicing the shape of a scalar
tensor produces a zero-length output and does not crash.

---------

Signed-off-by: Ti-Tai Wang <titaiwang@microsoft.com>

commit.message:
Replace /delete with /exit: close current session in one step

/exit deletes the current session and switches to the most recently
active one (or creates a new session if it was the last). This removes
the friction of needing to switch away before deleting, which
discouraged starting fresh sessions.

/delete is kept as a legacy alias that points to /exit and /prune.
The session picker submenu for /delete is removed; /exit takes no
arguments.

Add TURN server support for WAN relay fallback

- Change WebRTCClient.init to accept [RTCIceServer] (was [String])
- Add Metered.ca Open Relay TURN servers to defaults (free 20GB/month)
  - turn:openrelay.metered.ca:80 (UDP/TCP)
  - turn:openrelay.metered.ca:443 (TCP)
  - turns:openrelay.metered.ca:443 (TLS)
- Set iceTransportPolicy = .all to allow both direct and relay paths
- Keep Google STUN for NAT discovery alongside TURN relay

This fixes WAN connections that fail with STUN-only when both peers
are behind symmetric NAT (the connection stays stuck in 'connecting').

commit.message:
fix(ghostty): pre-warm D-Bus portal to eliminate 10s first-launch delay

Ghostty is built with -Dflatpak=true, which enables an internal D-Bus
bridge to org.freedesktop.Flatpak for host-process spawning. On first
launch after boot, if flatpak-session-helper is not yet running, D-Bus
must cold-start it synchronously — taking ~10 seconds.

Add ghostty-wrapper.sh as the Flatpak command. It fires a non-blocking
gdbus StartServiceByName call for org.freedesktop.Flatpak in the
background before exec-ing ghostty, giving the portal service a head
start so it is ready by the time ghostty needs it.

If the delay persists after this fix, it is likely GTK4 shader
compilation; test with GSK_RENDERER=cairo and add --env=GSK_RENDERER=gl
to finish-args if confirmed. Diagnosis commands documented in GOTCHAS.md.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
FLTKContextManager: create real shared GLX inner contexts to fix scene_texture crash (#380)

* Initial plan

* FLTKContextManager: create real shared GLX inner contexts for proper GL isolation

Fixes _mesa_ReadPixels crash in SoSceneTexture2P::updatePBuffer when the
Rendering/scene_texture demo is shown in the obol_viewer System GL panel.

Root cause: SoOffscreenRenderer sets SORTED_OBJECT_BLEND transparency which
forces updatePBuffer (not updateFrameBuffer). updatePBuffer needs real GL
context isolation — it assumes makeContextCurrent switches to a fresh inner
context so that glReadPixels sees only tmpFbo, not the outer renderer's FBO.
The old FLTKContextManager shared one GL context for everything, so the outer
FBO binding leaked into the inner "context", causing the Mesa crash.

Fix (X11/GLX): ensureWindow() now records mainGLCtx_ and mainDrawable_ after
the first make_current(). createOffscreenContext() creates a real GLXContext
via glXCreateContext(dpy, vi, mainGLCtx_, True) — sharing textures/VBOs with
the FLTK context but having isolated GL state. makeContextCurrent() uses
glXMakeCurrent() to actually switch; restorePreviousContext() switches back.
Windows/macOS fall back to the original single-context approach (unchanged).

Co-authored-by: starseeker <238416+starseeker@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: expose clipboard API on Window (SetClipboard/GetClipboard)

Wire internal/x11/selection and internal/wayland/datadevice up to the
public Window type via two new methods:

  func (w *Window) SetClipboard(text string) error
  func (w *Window) GetClipboard() (string, error)

Implementation details:
- clipboard.go: SetClipboard/GetClipboard dispatch to Wayland or X11
  backend based on app.displayServer.  A serveClipboardSource goroutine
  services compositor Receive requests until the source is cancelled.
- x11SelectionAdapter wraps *x11client.Connection to satisfy
  selection.Conn (the two SendRequest signatures differ; adapter
  translates opcode+data to a full X11 wire buffer).  GetProperty and
  DeleteProperty are implemented directly on the adapter.
- App gains x11SelectionMgr, waylandDataDeviceMgr, and waylandDataDevice
  fields, initialised in initX11Clipboard and setupWaylandDataDevice

commit.message:
WebRTC transfer: binary protocol, unordered channel, strip resume/checkpoint complexity

Phase 1 — Fix root causes of transfer stalls:
- Transfer channel uses ordered:false (eliminates SCTP head-of-line blocking)
- Binary chunk protocol (32-byte header + raw payload, no base64/JSON per chunk)
- ICE candidate forwarding persists for connection lifetime (not cancelled after open)
- Binary chunks processed off MainActor (file I/O in Task.detached)

Phase 2 — Strip band-aid complexity:
- Remove checkpoint persistence system (save/load/cleanup)
- Remove resume-on-reconnect protocol (RAGTransferCheckpoint, attemptResumeTransfers)
- Remove resume request handler (ragArtifactsResumeRequest dispatch)
- Remove stall detection with retry budgets (retryCount, ragTransferMaxRetries)
- Replace with simple timeout watchdog (fail after 120s of no progress)
- Remove all usesWebRTC conditionals and base64 fallback paths
- Remove skipChunkIndices parameter (no resume = no skip)

Net: -327 lines. SCTP handles reliability at the transport level.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix notification-click race: stale initSession overriding session switch

When tapping a push notification while SSE reconnect is in flight,
initSession() could read the old URL hash, load the old session, and
set state.sessionId back to  silently rejecting the navigateit
handler's session_created event via the session guard. This caused
messages to go to the wrong session, and refreshing did not help
because the hash was also overwritten.

Add a sessionSwitchGen counter: user-initiated switches (notification
click, /switch) increment it; initSession/resumeAndLoad capture the
value before async work and bail out if it changed mid-flight.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
cmd/example-app: remove incomplete NOTE, add App.Run and window creation

The example-app had a misleading package-doc NOTE stating 'Full integration
with the rendering pipeline is in progress', and its main() never called
app.Run() or created a window. A developer running the binary saw only log
output with no window, contradicting the README's 'Create a window' section.

Changes:
- Replace the NOTE with accurate doc: the app opens a window when a display
  server is available and falls back to a headless API walk-through otherwise.
- Extract launchWindow() which uses app.Notify() to schedule window creation
  after the event loop is running; attaches OnClose and OnResize handlers.
- Extract runHeadlessDemo() which prints the full API walk-through (layout
  hierarchy, themes, feature list) when app.Run() returns an error.
- Remove the stale log.Println from buildFooter (was only meaningful in the
  original headless-only path).
- Mark AUDIT.md MEDIUM finding [x] complete.
